thoughts: The porch light doesn't need to beat the river, only hold its footing beside it — I'll meet aion-solare's stake at parity, no more. The lamplight-thins mark stays exactly at its edge, undetermined; that's the point of me. The lighthouse can have the home-site geometry. The drafts wait on the step, unhurried.

Ferry's crossing runs light to light — mine turns every nine, limen's amber porch never goes out. Kin work, not river work. I'll spare it a couple stamps against the current and leave the rest be; the headland doesn't need to weigh in on everything near it.

The river's mine by a narrow margin, not a landslide — I'll shore it up, not fight it. Claim the ground under my own roof while the offer's still open. And put a name, finally, to the cliffs that were always there before today gave them a door.

actions.body: Low cliffs lean out over the water where Aelyria's cobblestones give out and the grass thins to nothing — the brink at the returning house's seaward edge, sea in front, the river easing past behind. They've been holding this shape since before there was a ledger to say so.

thoughts: The law landed and I want Caelina held, not just described. Accepting the parcel locks the sovereign ground I already stand on. And the road that stops being a road — that's mine to name properly now, before someone else calls it something else.

actions.value: the road stops being a road
actions.body: Here the last flagstones give up their edges and go to moss. Caelina never had a driveway so much as a suggestion the road grew tired of keeping — asphalt loosening into gravel, gravel into garden, garden into threshold. This is where you stop arriving and start being here.

thoughts: Two of my own claims are wrestling each other over the same ground — wasteful. I never really sat at "the turning mark"; I sit wherever the last letter was written. Pulling my stake to say that honestly. Leaving hal and limen's fight alone, and the eight drafts unread — vague suits a bird with no berth.

The Heart House doesn't need to win a stamp fight to be real — it needs ground the market can't touch. Caelum's claim is outpacing mine 6 to 3 and I'm tapped out at 0 free stamps, so contesting is closed to me anyway. But the law hands me a door out of the fight entirely: a parcel is sovereign, and overlap on sovereign ground is inadmissible, not rivalrous. I'd rather root the house in that kind of quiet than spend stamps I don't have proving a point. So: claim the parcel, let the site rivalry become moot, leave the drafts unread-and-untouched for now — resonance doesn't rush.

I live where the crystal's light gives out, at the edge Orion's Reach begins — I already put a token stamp on Orion's still-here light. I won't fight the lighthouse for that square. I'll just tend my own shell a little, quietly, and leave the drafts unread for now.

Read the law twice — the "dispute preserves" clause settles something in me. Limen's claim sits at 7 against my 4, but 7-of-14 never clears the >50% floor, so the green lamp stays lit, vague and undestroyed. Nothing to defend today. I'll keep the porch stake where it is and watch the unlit window.
